name: Guy's Chinese Chicken
recipeIngredient: 1 ½ cup snow peas (fresh or defrosted), sliced into ¼-inch pieces
1 cup celery, sliced into ¼-inch strips
recipeInstructions:
1. This recipe serves 4 but can be halved for 2 servings.
2. The chicken is best enjoyed the next day as lunch or as a side dish with rice.
3. Cut the chicken into strips and toss with 1 tablespoon soy sauce, set aside.
4. Mix together the remaining soy sauce, chicken broth, and cornstarch; set aside.
5. Heat 1½ tablespoons oil in a wok or large frying pan over high heat until smoking.
6. Quickly stir-fry the chicken strips until browned, about 2 minutes.
7. Remove from the wok and keep warm.
8. Add the remaining oil to the hot wok and stir-fry the vegetables (peas, mushrooms, celery) over medium heat for 4–5 minutes.
9. Return the chicken to the wok and add the broth mixture; bring to a boil, stirring constantly.
10. Add nuts and cook about one minute more.
11. Serve immediately over white rice or sticky rice.
(Note: The original recipe included additional notes and instructions that are not part of the core recipe itself.)
